channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Understanding the structure of swine leukocyte antigen class I molecules
item.description: This study examines the structural characteristics of swine leukocyte antigen (SLA) class I molecules, which are important for understanding immune recognition in swine. The research was conducted by investigators from Dalian University and affiliated institutions in China, with findings relevant to

item.title: Ciliation and mucus secretion in an in vitro model of nasal mucosa based on CI-pNaEC cell line for preclinical drug testing
item.description: Researchers developed an in vitro nasal mucosa model using the CI-pNaEC cell line to evaluate ciliation and mucus secretion for preclinical drug testing. This model system enables assessment of key functional characteristics of nasal tissue relevant to pharmaceutical development and safety evaluatio

item.title: Organoid-AI platforms need integrated governance in drug discovery
item.description: Organoid-AI platforms are functioning as integrated decision systems in drug discovery, influencing compound prioritization, toxicity assessment, and program progression. Current governance approaches validate the biological and computational components separately, despite their interdependent roles

item.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
item.description: Mistral AI, which offers some open source AI models, has raised significant funding since its creation in 2023, with the ambition to “put frontier AI in the hands of everyone.”

item.title: Oral microbiome modulation mitigates hyperglycemia exacerbation in gestational diabetes mellitus
item.description: Here, the authors associate changes in the oral microbiome during gestational diabetes with gum inflammation and blood sugar control, and show in animal models and a human trial that restoring healthier oral bacteria or topical docosahexaenoic acid, reduce th…

item.title: Daily current affairs ias | upsc prelims and mains exam – 29th june 2026
item.description: Archives (PRELIMS Focus) (MAINS Focus) The post DAILY CURRENT AFFAIRS IAS | UPSC Prelims and Mains Exam – 29th June 2026 appeared first on IASbaba.

item.title: The AI trade is losing one of its key signals
item.description: AI trade signals weaken as pricing power declines, raising concerns over future profitability and market sustainability.

item.title: The only AI glossary you'll need this year | techcrunch
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: Journalist karen hao on sam altman, openai & the "quasi-religious" push for artificial intelligence
item.description: As part of our July Fourth special broadcast, we continue our extended interview with Karen Hao, author of Empire of AI: Dreams and Nightmares in Sam Altman’s OpenAI. The book documents the rise of OpenAI and how the AI industry is leading to a new form of co…

item.title: Is AI a threat to healthcare jobs? which jobs are and aren’t at risk
item.description: Will AI replace healthcare jobs? Not exactly. Learn which roles face the greatest disruption, which remain resilient, and how the workforce is evolving.

item.title: Ibio expands cardiometabolic and obesity program with anti-myostatin antibody discovered using its proprietary platform, in-licensed from astralbio
item.description: iBio Expands Cardiometabolic and Obesity Program with Anti-Myostatin Antibody Discovered Using its Proprietary Platform, In-Licensed from AstralBioibioinc.com

item.title: Icas members call for bigger government role in AI regulation
item.description: An ICAS survey found that 60% of CAs want greater government intervention in AI regulation.

item.title: Openai offers feds a stake, anthropic gets out of AI model jail and meta wants to be a neocloud
item.description: OpenAI reportedly has floated giving the U.S. government a 5% stake in the company, perhaps the start of a series of such stakes in other AI companies as well. This no doubt has traditional anti-industrial-policy Republicans, all apparently dead now, spinning…

item.title: AI law may be considered as time is getting right, export curbs eased on mythos: meity secretary s krishnan
item.description: The Centre may begin work on a dedicated legal framework for artificial intelligence (AI) as the technology continues to evolve, Secretary, Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), S Krishnan, said on Friday, indicating that the government …

item.title: Cloud trust is becoming the next cybersecurity battleground for AI
item.description: Organisations increasingly need to prove that infrastructure, workloads and data environments remain trustworthy throughout their lifecycle. The post Cloud trust is becoming the next cybersecurity battleground for AI appeared first on Digital Journal.

item.title: FDA oral glp-1 vote extends obesity drug rally across biotech - bullishdaily
item.description: FDA support for an oral GLP-1 obesity drug strengthens the metabolic franchise trade, lifting obesity-focused biotech while intensifying large-cap pharma competition.

item.title: Frontiers | association between mental health service utilization and diabetes-hypertension comorbidity: a community-based study
item.description: ObjectiveTo examine the association between mental health service (MHS) utilization and diabetes–hypertension (DM–HTN) comorbidity and to explore the roles o...

item.title: Texas oil regulator directs doge to enhance rrc efficiency
item.description: 'Our goal is simple', RRC Commissioner Wayne Christian said.

item.title: The browser wars aren't about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to chrome and safari | techcrunch
item.description: The browser wars aren't about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
item.description: At the event "The Briefing: AI for Science" earlier this week, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a new "AI workbench for scientists" that pulls fragmented tools and datasets into one environment, and generates figures and visuals. Anthropic, already dominat…

item.title: AI bills are baffling the c-suite after shift to usage-based pricing
item.description: KPMG finds nearly a third of execs struggle to understand costs as companies rethink deployments

item.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
item.description: Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. But the detection methods being implemented are questionable, and any fanfic writer could be caught in the crossfire. Broad distaste around the u…

item.title: STAT+: A ‘historic’ FDA clearance raises the question: Is LLM the interface? Or the decision-maker?
item.description: The newly cleared device is based around an app that helps a patient manage their diabetes using a treatment plan defined by their doctor.

item.title: Takeda and insilico strike ai-led drug discovery deal worth up to $600m
item.description: HONG KONG -- Japanese pharmaceutical giant Takeda has reached a deal with Hong Kong-based Insilico Medicine to use the biotechnology company's artificial intelligence platform to identify potential new drugs. Collaboration underlines surge in cross-border bio…

item.title: How proteins fold
item.description: Despite advances in protein structure prediction, how proteins fold remains unclear. Understanding protein (mis)folding will require single-molecule techniques and advanced computation tools.

item.title: People behind the progress: preparing us for what comes next
item.description: The TechUK President’s Awards celebrate individuals across the tech sector who are helping to transform the world we live in for the better

item.title: Pre-deployment simulations are vital to developing generative AI that can best provide mental health advice
item.description: Pre-deployment simulation is a new technique from OpenAI. It can be used to better shape AI-led mental health guidance. An AI Insider analysis and scoop.

item.title: 5 under-the-radar stocks fueling the AI revolution
item.description: Nvidia was the first poster child of the AI revolution. OpenAI turned ChatGPT into a global phenomenon. Google, Microsoft, and Anthropic have since dominated headlines with ever-more-powerful models. But throughout history, the biggest fortunes made during a …

item.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30m of his own money to build AI alternative to microsoft office | techcrunch
item.description: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office, Google Apps with AI.

item.title: UN warns of need for global governance to avoid an ai-pocalypse
item.description: Capabilities are racing ahead of rules to ensure tech is used safely and responsibly, Scientific Panel argues

item.title: Bacillus Calmette-Guérin (BCG) immunotherapy reprograms CNS immunity and alters Alzheimer's biomarkers: results from two open-label clinical trials
item.description: Two open-label clinical trials demonstrate that Bacillus Calmette-Guérin (BCG) immunotherapy modulates central nervous system immunity and produces measurable changes in Alzheimer's disease biomarkers. These findings suggest BCG may alter the immune environment in the brain through a reprogramming m

item.title: Dissecting autonomous enzymatic variability in single cells
item.description: Researchers used single-cell analysis to characterize the inherent variability in enzyme activity across individual cells, providing insights into how enzymatic function differs at the cellular level independent of external factors.

item.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
item.description: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.

item.title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
item.description: Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.

item.title: The Download: a startup has a solution for AI’s groupthink problem
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give m

item.title: 15 examples of real-world challenges: insights from the aws summit washington, d.c. event
item.description: As organizations race to operationalize generative and agentic artificial intelligence, the conversation is shifting from pilots and proofs of concept to real-world AI deployments that deliver measurable business outcomes. Success increasingly depends on comb…

item.title: Anthropic’s fable model is back. but u.s. AI policy is still a mess
item.description: The U.S. government has lifted export controls on Fable and Mythos. But there's still no clarity about the rules that will govern future AI model releases.

item.title: Uktech50 2026 winner: demis hassabis, co-founder and CEO, deepmind
item.description: Third-time UKtech50 winner Demis Hassabis has shown what homegrown UK tech talent can do, and the importance of tech for good

item.title: Usc scientists target brain inflammation to prevent alzheimer's disease
item.description: An interdisciplinary team at the USC Center for Personalized Brain Health (CPBH) is targeting a family of enzymes that can increase the risk of inflammation in the brains of people with the APOE4 gene variation-the most significant genetic risk factor for lat…

item.title: Doctors miss about 12 million diagnoses per year — here is what AI can actually fix
item.description: 12 million missed diagnoses per year, 40,000–80,000 preventable deaths. AI is helping most in imaging. Here's where the evidence supports AI diagnostic assistance — and where it doesn't.

item.title: America at 250: navigating u.s. AI policy and the digital overhaul of the textile industry
item.description: Discover how AI is transforming the textile industry with automation, sustainability, and evolving U.S. regulatory policies.

item.title: Show hn: healthchain – python sdk to connect AI models to live EHR systems
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/healthchainai/HealthChain Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48764545 Points: 1 # Comments: 0

item.title: Study finds health and safety is rapidly evolving from a compliance function to a strategic business driver
item.description: VelocityEHS has released a landmark industry study revealing how… The post Study finds health and safety is rapidly evolving from a compliance function to a strategic business driver appeared first on OHS Canada Magazine.

item.title: Every hour spent sitting down increases the risk of cancer by nearly a tenth
item.description: Each extra hour sitting down may raise your risk of deadly cancer by nearly a tenth, a study from researchers at the University of Glasgow suggests.

item.title: You gotta get up to get your cancer risk down, new study finds
item.description: New research suggests prolonged periods of sedentary behavior are uniquely harmful to our health.

item.title: Deploy modern data platforms in minutes with mdaa
item.description: In this post, we explore how MDAA transforms data architecture development from months of manual coding to production-ready deployment through configuration-driven infrastructure and embedded governance, examine a real customer transformation, and provide a c…

item.title: Is the homeopathic industry “on the ropes”?
item.description: The U.S. homeopathic industry is under growing regulatory and legal pressure. The post Is the Homeopathic Industry “On the Ropes”? first appeared on Science-Based Medicine.

item.title: Growth monitoring and promotion uptake and its associated factors among mothers/ caregivers of children aged under two years in wondo district, ethiopia: cross-sectional study
item.description: Introduction Growth monitoring and promotion (GMP) can be used to address developmental halting before a child’s health deteriorates. However, there hasn’t been much research done on the GMP uptake in the Wondo district. Objective The study aimed to evaluat…

item.title: Mobility data resolution needed to inform predictive models of spatial epidemic spread from mobile phone data
item.description: Author summary Human mobility patterns play a crucial role in the spatial spread of infectious diseases, as pathogens move between communities through the movements of infected individuals. During the COVID-19 pandemic, mobile phone companies shared unprecede…

item.title: Incidence of dengue fever in pakistan
item.description: Introduction Dengue is a growing health threat in Pakistan due to climate, urbanization, and inadequate sanitation. Despite recurrent seasonal outbreaks, comprehensive evidence on the dengue burden in Pakistan remains limited due to due to underreporting, inc…
